channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kerala Labour Laws and Retrenchment Rules: Government to Address Legal Concerns
item.description: The article discusses the recent retrenchment of approximately 800 employees by the US-based tech firm CorroHealth Infotech Private Limited in Kerala, sparking legal debates. The company cited operati...

item.title: Inquest opened into death of Bafana midfielder Jayden Adams
item.description: Cape Town police have initiated an inquest following the tragic death of 25-year-old Bafana Bafana and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams, whose body was discovered at a property in Schotsche K...

item.title: Canberra's Riley Hamilton earns AFL Rising Star nomination after standout debut
item.description: Riley Hamilton, a 19-year-old rookie with the GWS Giants, has earned an AFL Rising Star nomination following a standout performance in his second game. The Canberra product made his AFL debut in an up...

item.title: Sam Neill: A Versatile Actor's Legacy in Cinema
item.description: Sam Neill, the acclaimed New Zealand-born actor, passed away at 76, leaving behind a legacy of iconic roles and contributions to cinema. Renowned for his versatility, Neill's career spanned over five ...

item.title: HDFC Bank Reduces Staff Amid Automation Push to Boost Efficiency
item.description: HDFC Bank, India's largest private lender, has reduced its workforce by 3,343 employees in the last financial year as part of an aggressive automation initiative. The bank's total staff decreased to 2...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari Links Kenyans to Gulf Job Opportunities
item.description: Prophet Victor Kanyari, a controversial Kenyan preacher, announced job opportunities in Gulf countries like Dubai, Kuwait, Bahrain, Abu Dhabi, Saudi Arabia, and Oman during a Sunday church service. He...

item.title: Nigerian Students Shouldn't Bear WAEC/NECO Fees – Aisha Yesufu Advocates Free Education
item.description: Aisha Yesufu, a prominent Nigerian activist, has strongly opposed the proposed ₦50,000 fee hike for WAEC and NECO exams, calling it unjust. The Federal Government initially approved the increase, effe...

item.title: News Corp Australia addresses bot detection issue
item.description: The article discusses News Corp Australia's technical measures to manage crawler bot traffic on their websites. Users experiencing access issues as non-bot readers or subscribers are advised to tempor...

item.title: Alumni urged to invest in school development for future generations
item.description: The article discusses the role of alumni in supporting school development, highlighting the efforts of Tambaza Secondary School's 1994-1996 graduates who renovated sanitation facilities to benefit fem...

item.title: Senate Resumes Impeachment Trial of VP Sara Duterte as Proceedings Enter Second Week
item.description: The Senate impeachment court resumed the trial of Vice President Sara Duterte on July 13, 2026, entering its second week of proceedings. Both senator-judges and the House prosecution panel are pushing...

item.title: Ontario government defends privacy concerns over ministers' speeding fines
item.description: The Ontario government has argued that revealing the names of cabinet ministers whose vehicles were caught speeding would violate personal privacy and expose their travel habits. Freedom of informatio...

item.title: Heineken Introduces Bar De Change for Nigerian Travelers
item.description: Heineken has launched Bar De Change, Nigeria's first social currency exchange, allowing Nigerians traveling to the United States and Mexico to enjoy Heineken abroad at home prices. The initiative lets...

item.title: Bluesky's Toni Schneider Becomes Permanent CEO After Four Months as Interim Leader
item.description: Toni Schneider, the founding CEO of Automattic (parent company of WordPress and Tumblr), has transitioned from interim CEO to permanent chief executive of Bluesky. Schneider, who took over after Jay G...

item.title: UK Implements Hosepipe Ban Amid Ongoing Heatwave and Drought Concerns
item.description: The UK has imposed widespread hosepipe bans across multiple regions due to an ongoing heatwave and rising drought risks. Record-breaking temperatures, including the eighth consecutive day above 34°C i...

item.title: Proposed Group Format for Football League to Address Competitive Balance Issues
item.description: A veteran football administrator known as 'Mzee wa Fact' has suggested restructuring the country's top-tier football league into a group-stage format, similar to the lower division system used in 2007...

item.title: Khampepe Commission Continues Inquiry into Apartheid Perpetrators
item.description: The Khampepe commission of inquiry, tasked with investigating alleged efforts to stall the prosecution of apartheid-era perpetrators, is continuing its proceedings in Johannesburg on Monday, July 13, ...

item.title: One Nation's popularity surge shows signs of slowing amid shifting political dynamics
item.description: Recent opinion polls indicate that One Nation's surge in popularity, which followed the Bondi terror attack in December 2025, is beginning to wane. While the party's primary vote dropped to 26% in the...

item.title: South Korean Ex-President Sentenced to Two Years for Political Funding Violations
item.description: A South Korean court has sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to two years in prison after finding him guilty of illegally receiving opinion polling services worth 270 million won ($179,800) from ...

item.title: Plea Deal Collapse in SAPS Tender Corruption Case
item.description: The plea deal between the Investigative Directorate Against Corruption (Idac) and alleged criminal cartel member Vusimusi 'Cat' Matlala has collapsed. Matlala had previously agreed to an eight-year di...

item.title: Joaquin Tovera Transfers to La Salle After Leaving UP
item.description: Joaquin Tovera, a prominent basketball player, has officially committed to joining La Salle University after his departure from the University of the Philippines (UP). This decision marks a significan...
